summary: Decompiled a large slice of the Item Switch menu unit, adding static data tables, typed implementations, and header prototypes for mnitemsw UI/input/GObj code. The PR mattered because decomp.dev reported +1272 bytes of newly matched code, including exact matches for mnItemSw_80233B68, mnItemSw_80235020, and mnItemSw_80233A98, plus several 93-99% near-matches in the same unit. The PR body and human review context were minimal, so intent beyond tracking/matching is inferred from the diff and bot report.

key_files.role: Added 812 lines implementing Item Switch menu static tables, input handling, UI animation/update logic, item JObj creation, and menu GObj construction.
Replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures, changed mnItemSw_802351A0 to return HSD_GObj*, and exported mnItemSw_804D6BE8.

what_changed: Added MnItemSwTable with x00 animation state triples, x30 animation/loop values, 32 item animation values, and a 32-entry item_order map, plus the two-value toggle animation table mnItemSw_804D4BA0.
Implemented mnItemSw_80233A98, including JP/non-JP language exceptions for item IDs 17 and 19, and used a dont_inline pragma around it.

Implemented mnItemSw_80233B68 to update mn_804A04F0.hovered_selection and confirmed_selection for directional button bits, including special non-item selections 0x1F and 0x20 and two-column wrapping behavior.

Added fn_80233E10 input handling for Back, A, Start, and directional movement; it writes item settings through mn_8022E978, updates gmMainLib_8015CC58()->item_freq, calls lb_8001CE00 for persistence, and routes/destroys menu GObjs.

Added near-matched UI lifecycle/update functions mnItemSw_80234104, mnItemSw_8023453C, fn_80234C24, and mnItemSw_802351A0 to build item switch JObjs, position the cursor across two columns, create SIS text labels, animate item toggles/highlights, and synchronize cached MnItemSwData with global menu state.
Added mnItemSw_80235020 to create an item entry JObj from MenMainCursorIs_Top, attach animations, set icon/toggle state, and show or hide the selected highlight.
Updated mnitemsw.h prototypes from unknown placeholders to concrete types for MnItemSwData*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, u8, and u32 arguments/returns.
smart_moves: Centralizing item animation values, ordering, and loop data in MnItemSwTable made the .data shape explicit and let multiple functions index the same table consistently.
The code keeps uncertain semantics as offset-style fields such as x00, x30, x21, and x23, while naming evidenced fields like items, item_order, cursor, menu_kind, and jobjs.
Special cursor IDs 0x1F and 0x20 are handled explicitly or with unsigned range checks, separating item-list entries 0x00-0x1E from item frequency/special controls.
The implementation mirrors real HSD object lifetimes: GObj_Create, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObj_SetupProc, HSD_MemAlloc, GObj_InitUserData, and HSD_Free.
Change detection compares cached MnItemSwData cursor/items/x21 against mn_804A04F0 before playing move SFX or updating animations, avoiding unnecessary UI work and matching original menu flow.
The diff preserves matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK uses, explicit casts, pointer iteration over item_order, and an HSD_ASSERTREPORT with the original line number/string.
decomp_lessons: For menu decomp, recover static animation/order tables early; they often control both codegen and .data matching across several functions.
Typed headers should be advanced alongside implementations: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS exposed real caller contracts and corrected mnItemSw_802351A0 from void to HSD_GObj*.
Menu code often maintains both global UI state and per-GObj cached state; exact-ish matches depend on preserving when the cache is compared, updated, and written back.
Sentinel indices beyond the normal array range can represent non-item controls. Here 0x1F and 0x20 must not be treated like data->items indices.
Even partial UI decomp is useful when exact leaf functions are landed first: the bot showed three 100% matches while larger UI procs remained 93-99% and could be finished later.
Language-dependent asset/animation remaps belong in small helper functions such as mnItemSw_80233A98 rather than being duplicated at call sites.
naming_conventions: Module symbols retain address-style names such as mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_80233B68, mnItemSw_80235020, and mnItemSw_802351A0.
Unnamed callbacks remain fn_80233E10 and fn_80234C24 rather than receiving speculative descriptive names.
Globals/static data use the project address suffix convention: mnItemSw_803ED340, mnItemSw_804D4BA0, mnItemSw_804D6BE8, and mnItemSw_804D6BEC.
Asset externs preserve source symbol casing, including MenMainCursorIs_Top and MenMainConIs_Top.
Struct fields with uncertain meaning stay offset-like, while strongly evidenced array/role names are used for items, item_order, cursor, menu_kind, and jobjs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` around mnItemSw_80233A98 to preserve the expected call boundary.
Inserted `PAD_STACK(0x20)` in fn_80233E10 and `PAD_STACK(0x18)` in fn_80234C24 for stack-shape matching.
Used unsigned casts and range idioms such as `(u8)(sel - 0x1F) <= 1U`, `(u8)i < 0x1F`, and `(s32) gm_801A4310() == 1` to match compiler behavior.
Pointer-walked `item_order` in loops instead of always indexing, matching register/dataflow patterns visible in the decompiled code.
Casted portions of `tbl->x30` to `AnimLoopSettings*` for mn_8022ED6C, preserving a shared data layout instead of inventing separate typed objects.

decomp.dev evidence: exact matches for mnItemSw_80233B68 (+680 bytes), mnItemSw_80235020 (+384), and mnItemSw_80233A98 (+208); near-matches for mnItemSw_802351A0, mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, and fn_80233E10.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice; the PR body only said "Tracking PR as usual".
decomp-dev bot reported matched code at 62.21%, up +0.03% and +1272 bytes.
Bot-reported exact new matches: mnItemSw_80233B68 at 100%, mnItemSw_80235020 at 100%, and mnItemSw_80233A98 at 100%.

Bot-reported near-match improvements remained unfinished: mnItemSw_802351A0 98.18%, mnItemSw_8023453C 98.88%, mnItemSw_80234104 97.33%, fn_80234C24 97.57%, fn_80233E10 93.41%, plus .data/.sdata/.sdata2 improvements.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish exact matching mnItemSw_802351A0, mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, and fn_80233E10.
Search for renames or documentation of MnItemSwData fields x21 and x23 and MnItemSwTable fields x00 and x30.
Audit other mn menu screens for the same StaticModelDesc plus HSD_JObjAddChild item-list construction pattern used by mnItemSw_80235020 and mnItemSw_802351A0.
Look for follow-up work improving mnitemsw .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 from the partial bot percentages reported here.
Compare handling of special selections 0x1F/0x20 and gmMainLib_8015CC58()->item_freq with mnmainrule and other rules-menu decomp.
